reason: ndim-aware mask already present: mask = domain_ok if base_arr.ndim==1 else domain_ok[:,None]; multi-base broadcast crash fixed.
In integrator resolved list (per-model y-clip envelope leak).
predict_quantile now branches on transform.requires_base and skips base extraction for unary transforms (lines 280-308).
X_valid = X if bool(valid.all()) else self._subset_rows(...) РІР‚вЂќ no-copy passthrough on all-valid mask present.
_transform now raises RuntimeError with context instead of swallowing and returning raw X.

predict_quantile still threads no groups kwarg and never drops group_column, so grouped-transform quantile prediction crashes; from_fitted_inner also lacks group_column. Multi-touch plumbing + tests for a niche grouped+quantile combo; out of scope for a quick safe pass.

TypeError-based sample_weight retry at 3 sites (_estimator.py:494-504,572-582; post_shim.py:62-68) mis-attributes deep TypeErrors as 'no sample_weight support' -> silent unweighted training. Correct fix is signature-gating via has_fit_parameter/inspect.signature, a behavior change needing a test matrix.

feature_importances_/coef_/intercept_/booster_ still use getattr(inner, attr, None), so a fitted inner lacking the attr yields hasattr==True with value None (hasattr-trap). Switching to a no-default raise is a behavior change that can break callers relying on None; needs a deliberate change + tests.

Pre-fit predict/predict_quantile raise bespoke RuntimeError (lines 25-28, 265-268); sklearn convention is NotFittedError, which subclasses ValueError+AttributeError so existing broad catches still work and hasattr-probing code sees it correctly.

from_fitted_inner reconstructs the T-clip as symmetric +/-10*std(y) (centered at 0), mis-centering unary transforms (log_y/cbrt_y/yeo_johnson_y) whose T is offset from 0 -> in-distribution T_hat clipped flat. Fix recomputes T_train via transform.forward(y,zeros,params) + MAD logic; numerics change needing a biz_value regression.

For grouped transforms feature_names_in_ includes group_column (F) but n_features_in_ delegates to the inner fit on F-1 cols, breaking the sklearn invariant. Fix must rework the class-level n_features_in_ property to prefer a stored wrapper value across both fit and from_fitted_inner routes; not a one-liner (instance attr cannot shadow the class property).
_drop_columns uses pandas X.drop which materialises a full copy per grouped predict on 100+GB frames; CoW/lazy-subset fix requires a before/after benchmark per perf-measure-first.

update() refit refreshes only alpha/beta, leaving y_clip/t_clip/y_train_median at pre-drift values so corrected predictions get clipped back toward the old regime. Refreshing the envelopes is a corrective-mechanism change (default-on) needing a regression test.

predict_quantile lacks domain-mask/fallback, T-clip, and runtime_stats/callback parity with predict (NaN base -> silent NaN quantiles). Requires factoring out the shared mask+fallback+clip+counters block and reuse per quantile column; refactor + tests.

fallback_predict is only validated lazily on the first predict batch hitting a domain violation (possibly weeks into prod); sklearn convention is validate-in-fit. Cheap membership check. (The unary len(y)!=len(X) check is left to a future pass since a frame-type X-row-count branch is needed.)

_extract_base docstring + TypeError message (lines 87,114-117) promise 'structured ndarray with named columns' but no ndarray path exists (any ndarray raises TypeError); _extract_base_matrix ndarray fallback (189-191) delegates to it and also raises. Correct the docs/message rather than implement the dead path.

T-clip hits (t_low_hits/t_high_hits) are logged at WARNING every batch then discarded; not in runtime_stats_/callback. Adds callback-payload keys + a warn-throttle (contract change), better as a deliberate change.

@dataclass(frozen=True) auto-generates __hash__/__eq__ over fitted_params: dict; hash(spec) raises TypeError (unhashable dict) and spec_a==spec_b raises 'truth value of an array is ambiguous' when params hold ndarrays (median_residual/monotonic_residual). Exclude the field from eq/hash; also drop unused Dict/Tuple imports.

shim estimator_ property returns the unfitted inner pre-fit so check_is_fitted(shim) passes on an unfitted shim; shim also delegates only fit/predict, dropping predict_quantile for a quantile-capable nested member. Adds __sklearn_is_fitted__ + conditional quantile delegation; small feature, deliberate.

Verified-dead imports: __init__.py warnings/deque/BaseEstimator/RegressorMixin/clone/NotFittedError/Callable/Dict/List + module logger never used; _estimator.py math/deque/NotFittedError unused (only in comments/strings). Plus an undocumented pickling caveat for runtime_stats_callback closures.

Compliance matrix exercises only diff/pandas/LinearRegression; uncovered rows (unary/multi-base/grouped/polars/quantile/fallback) are exactly where E3/E6 crash. Test expansion (parametrize _make + clone/get_params/fit/predict per row).

update() boxes the whole deque to PyFloats via .tolist() and rebuilds two ndarrays every call even when the z-check short-circuits. Preallocated float64 ring-buffer redesign needs a benchmark (perf-measure-first).

update() allowlist is ('linear_residual',) only; linear_residual_robust shares forward/inverse and the {alpha,beta} param shape (registry.py:286-301) so the closed-form refit applies. Extending it + choosing robust-fit-on-buffer is a behavior choice needing a biz_value/test.

predict_quantile_ensemble single-batched-call PROBE catches only (TypeError, ValueError); a library-specific error (LightGBMError/CatBoostError/XGBoostError) from a member's predict_quantile crashes the whole ensemble instead of using the documented per-alpha scalar fallback. Broadening the catch affects only the optimization probe, not the strict per-alpha contract.

_YJ_INV_BASE_FLOOR=1e-12 floor applied in all three YJ-inverse paths (numpy/numba/scalar); the lambda-asymptote NaN can no longer reach predict() or the _screening_tiny.py:682 inverse. Matches pre-supplied resolved list.

Condition number now computed on unit-L2-normalised centered columns (base_scaled=base_centered/col_norms) -> BKW scaled condition index, invariant to units; mixed-scale independent bases no longer falsely declared collinear.

s = m*var_noise via Rice/von-Neumann lag-1 noise-variance estimator replaces m*std(signal); oversmoothing fixed. NOTE registry.py:621 description text is now stale (still says s=n_unique*std(y)*1.0) -- fold into the T3 cleanup.

_ratio_inverse multiplies by RAW base while _ratio_forward:313 floors with safe_base; in-domain rows 0<|base|<eps break round-trip with ~unbounded relative error. Mirroring the floor is bit-identical except on already-pathological near-zero rows.

_centered_ratio_inverse multiplies by RAW (base+c) while _centered_ratio_forward:150 floors `shifted`; same eps-asymmetry round-trip break. Only the eps-floor half is the safe fix; the sign-flip-below-train-envelope half needs params-aware domain (deferred to T15).
_rolling_quantile_ratio_inverse multiplies by RAW roll_med while forward:365 floors with `safe`; third instance of the T4 eps-asymmetry class.

James-Stein sigma2 proxy drops the Var(base_g) term (OLS slope variance is sigma2/(n_g*Var(base))), making the shrinkage factor unit-dependent. Real, but changing the proxy shifts which groups shrink -> needs a biz_value/regression measurement, not a blind edit.

After shrinking alpha_g the code never re-centres beta_g, so the per-group residual mean drifts from 0 (manufactured per-group bias). Fix changes residualisation numerics and needs the 'residual mean ~=0 after shrink' biz_value test to pin both sides.
Both degenerate early-returns of _monotonic_residual_fit omit is_degenerate/var_explained, so the MOST degenerate fits bypass the discovery/_eval.py:66 degeneracy gate that exists exactly for them.

Auto-knot rule n_unique//200 conflates cardinality with discreteness -> continuous bases on small/mid n get 3-5 knots instead of 12 and underfit. Re-tuning the divisor/predicate alters fitted splines on real configs; threshold change needs a benchmark.

_rolling_median bottleneck path diverges from pandas fallback (even-k off-by-one everywhere; odd-k tail constant-filled), so rolling_quantile_ratio T is non-reproducible across envs. Fixing the shift + truncated-centred tail medians changes T-values and needs a backend-parity test across even/odd k + boundaries; docstring claim 'consistent with min_periods=1 semantics' (line 822) is false and must be corrected in the same change.

Centred rolling window reads FUTURE base rows -> look-ahead in time-ordered deployment. Already gated out of default discovery for ordering reasons; the trailing-mode extension is FUTURE, but the immediately-safe step is a loud look-ahead caveat in the registry description.

Domain-filter compacts the row sequence before time-recurrent forwards (ewma/rolling/frac_diff), so train T near gaps differs from predict-time T computed on the full frame. Fix needs a TAG_SEQUENTIAL tag + full-length NaN-aware forward then mask -- architectural.

frac_diff is y-only but registered with default requires_base=True: discovery emits one IDENTICAL spec per base (misnamed y-fdiff-lag1/lag2/...) and drops rows on non-finite UNUSED base, compacting the y sequence (compounds T13).

Fitted-params-dependent domains are unenforceable: the pre-fit gate (discovery/_eval.py:44) can't see offset/c, so log_y screen rows below -offset NaN and T5's base+c<=0 rows pass as valid. Fix = new domain_check_fitted(y,base,params) hook on the Transform contract + wiring in screening/eval -- contract extension.

_yj_forward dispatch docstring claims 'both paths produce bit-identical output' but the forward numba kernel is fastmath=True (max diff ~1e-13). Docstring contradicts code. (Inverse kernel is fastmath=False and genuinely bit-identical -- leave it.)

Dead duplicate _ewma_kernel + _frac_diff_inverse_kernel and _MAD_FLOOR_FRAC/_MAD_SOFT_CAP_K in linear.py; live kernels are in nonlinear.py and live constants are lazy-imported from the parent at linear.py:101. Grep-confirmed NOT re-exported (the `from .linear import` lists in __init__.py:245 + registry.py:55 omit them). Drift risk.

The _finite_mask kwarg's comments advertise an 'outer Transform.fit dispatcher precompute' that NO call site uses (discovery/_eval.py:56 calls transform.fit(y_train[valid], base_train[valid]) plain). Threading it is a measurement-gated perf win (FUTURE); the immediately-honest fix is to correct the comments so they don't describe infrastructure that doesn't exist.

Transform contract says fitted-params dict 'is JSON-serialisable' but median/quantile/monotonic/rank/smoothing_spline/quantile_normal return ndarray values; json.dumps crashes. Amend the contract docstring ('values may be ndarrays; serialise via the spec serialiser') rather than mass-.tolist().

is_composite_target_name substring match (-diff-,-ratio-,-spline-,...) false-positives plausible user columns (price-diff-7d, debt-ratio-q). Strict parsing must stay robust to both short-alias and legacy __-format names -> behaviour change with pickle-compat surface, not a trivial safe edit.

Unary (requires_base=False) specs are named with the spurious current-loop base (y-cbrtY-lag1); misleading + makes dedup keys/report labels base-order-dependent. Fix (sentinel base + 2-segment is_composite_target_name form) changes emitted spec names -> touches dedup keys, report labels, possibly v1 pickles -> needs care.

except Exception: pass around the v2 pandas-groupby fast paths (simple.py:164 + nonlinear.py:276) silently reroutes to v1 forever on a real v2 defect -- the silent-error-swallowing class the project tracks.

logratio soft-cap makes the inverse inexact for in-domain train rows beyond 10*MAD (|T-med|>10*MAD). Deliberate safety tradeoff but the registry description omits the round-trip consequence and no test pins the capped branch. Add one sentence to the description (DOC); the two-sided test is a nice-to-have.

EWMA/frac-diff inverse cold-starts every predict batch from the train-mean anchor, biasing the first ~k rows where recency matters most. Statelessness is deliberate; warm_start/base_history is a new feature -> FUTURE.

Adversarial round-trip/parity coverage missing for the whole bug class. The RESOLVE_NOW items (T4/T5/T6/T9/T14) MUST ship their own regression tests in the same change; the remaining adversarial suite (YJ-asymptote, bottleneck parity for T11, grouped residual-mean for T8, mixed-scale non-fallback) is broader than this safe pass -> FUTURE.

NaN-bearing columns are now recomputed exactly in float64 with per-pair finite masking (col_has_nan loop); the mean-imputation dilution that defeated the leak gate is gone. On integrator ALREADY_RESOLVED list and confirmed in source.
iter_transform now column_stacks (base_column,*extra_base_columns) for multi-base specs before domain_check/forward, so linear_residual_multi no longer raises. On ALREADY_RESOLVED list and confirmed.
Multi-base promotion NaN-row masking fix landed per the integrator ALREADY_RESOLVED list.

max(hint_strengths[:len(hint_kept)]) indexes RAW-ordered strengths after filtered hints are dropped, so a dropped strong hint's % is misattributed to surviving weak hints (full-hint trust + demoter/dedup immunity granted wrongly). hint_raw and hint_strengths are positionally aligned, so it is fixable in-file without changing the list contract.

a1-a2 is a difference of two independent half-fits (Var=2*sigma^2/(half*var_base)) but se_alpha is the SE of a SINGLE half, inflating every drift z by sqrt(2)~1.41x -> excess WARN/over-reject at threshold 3.0. ENS-Low-2 fixed the sigma numerator, not this difference-variance factor.

per_stratum overshoot is truncated by out.sort();out[:sample_n], which deletes the excess exclusively from the LARGEST (latest) row indices - a systematic temporal bias that contradicts the function's own sort-for-temporal-fidelity rationale. With heavy-tail boost (n_strata=30) up to ~sample_n/30 latest rows are excised.

fit_with_stability_check does self.config.random_state=base_seed+i*7919 on the SHARED config; the heavy-tail boost in fit() swaps self.config for a model_copy mid-loop, so the line-236 restore writes the COPY, leaving the caller's original config permanently reseeded (poisons later targets + the cache-key config signature).

report_ kept=True is frozen at the eps gate; later alpha-drift/linres-collapse/rerank drops and multi-base name-swaps make report() actively misleading vs its 'all evaluated candidates with reasons' contract. Real, but the fix is a reconciliation pass (per-gate drop reasons + synthetic upgrade rows) plus tests - not a safe micro-fix.

Two seeds converging on the same multi-base set produce two specs (name 'X+Y' vs 'Y+X') for one identical joint-OLS transform; no set-level dedup means 2x downstream training and two perfectly-correlated ensemble members.

The mi_y per-base recompute is ALREADY fixed (lines 387-397 derive each base's mean via _aggregate_mi_per_feature(np.delete(_per_feat_y_full,...))). The remaining np.delete full-matrix copies kept alive for all bases need an exclude_col param threaded into _mi_to_target_prebinned plus a RAM/bench check - out of scope for a quick safe pass.

_mi_to_target knn path uses a global isfinite(target)&all(isfinite(X),axis=1) mask; one 99%-NaN column can drop <50 rows and zero MI for the whole sweep (silent degeneration of the documented heavy-tail mi_estimator='knn' option). Proper fix is per-column knn handling (whole-matrix->per-column loop, changes algorithm/perf) or a calibrated >X%%-NaN drop threshold - needs measurement.

The all-column AND finite-mask shrinks the MI ranking sample to rows where EVERY feature is observed (MNAR selection bias) for mid-range-NaN columns. Real, but the correct fix is a per-column _mi_pair_bin fallback when the AND-mask retains <~50%% of rows - a behavior change needing the same per-column rework as D11 plus calibration.

Unary (requires_base=False) transforms are deduped to the first base, scored against THAT base's x_remaining (a column the transform never uses) and stamped with its name; mi_gain shifts with irrelevant base reordering and the spec claims a fake base dependence. Fix = dedicated unary context (full X, base sentinel) spanning _fit/_eval/spec/name layers - a real refactor + tests.

df[col].to_numpy(dtype=np.float32) on a pandas nullable extension dtype (Int64/Float64/boolean) containing NA raises ValueError; _is_numeric_column approves those dtypes, so discovery crashes at the first column pull instead of treating NA as NaN.

A boolean-mask train_idx (common numpy idiom) is accepted silently then detonates later with a cryptic IndexError (_sample_indices reads mask LENGTH as row count; base_train[sample_idx] indexes positionally); float dtype is reinterpreted silently. The error names neither train_idx nor the cause.

The class docstring promises val/test integrity checks ('Leakage discipline (CRITICAL)') but fit performs none; overlapping train/test indices silently fit transform params, MI screens and tiny-rerank on test rows - exactly the leakage the class documents as its core discipline. The check is O(n log n), negligible vs MI screening.

Comment says 'Log first per-spec failure' but the guard is if b==0:, so bootstrap failures at replicates 1..N-1 are silent; the LCB is then computed over a reduced/biased sample with no operator signal.

y_train (219), y_train_for_strat (290) and _y_train_local (724) are the same y_full[train_idx] fancy-index copy - three O(n_train) gathers per fit; line 724 also has a dead 'if y_full is not None else' branch (y_full always bound by then).

std_y=float(np.std(y_train[isfinite])) or 1.0 is NaN-truthy on an empty/all-NaN slice (bool(NaN) is True), so std_y becomes NaN and silently disables the linres->diff collapse. Practically unreachable today but a guard-pattern bug that invites copy-paste.

self._df_ref=df pins a 100+GB frame on the fitted instance with no __getstate__ exclusion; pickling/deep-copy serialises the whole frame and pins it against GC, violating the project runtime-caches-break-pickle rule.

A ~0.99-correlated sibling left in X_remaining inflates mi_y_compare while contributing little to mi_t, biasing mi_gain DOWN (conservative, no leak) for exactly the lag-family bases most likely genuine. Optional accuracy improvement gated on auto_base_dedup_corr_threshold; needs a per-base column mask (ties to D10 exclude-col) + biz_value measurement.

The else 'base outside the FE pool' arm (lines 376-379) is unreachable - every base in usable_features is in _col_index - AND methodologically wrong (the base would sit in its own x_remaining set). Dead, silently-wrong code.

The degenerate-sample fallback returns list(usable_features)[:top_k], discarding hint_kept - the one signal (BD ablation) NOT derived from the broken screening sample, and hint features may not be within the first top_k of the arbitrary column order.

For 50<=n<5*mi_nbins every bin-MI value is silently 0.0 (mi_pair_bin/_mi_to_target_prebinned floor), so top-K degenerates to the alphabetical tiebreaker; undocumented at runtime. Log it; do not auto-shrink nbins (that changes numerics).

The gate admits on mi_gain_lcb but top-K ranking sorts on point s.mi_gain, so a high-variance big-point/low-LCB spec can outrank a stable better-LCB one - inconsistent with the gate's noise-floor rationale. Opt-in only (default bootstrap_n=0). The clean fix needs threading the entry-level lcb to the spec-sort (lcb is not on the spec), so document the intentional point-estimate ranking as a known minor inconsistency rather than ship a non-trivial plumbing change in a safe pass.

Already guarded: _rerank_inner_n_jobs=max(1,cpu//_rerank_n_jobs) threaded into _build_tiny_model(inner_n_jobs=). The 256-thread oversubscription path no longer exists; raw-baseline multiseed runs serially after the parallel loop (n_jobs default 1).
Min-seeds Wilcoxon guard present (_min_seeds_wilcoxon=ceil(log2(1/alpha)), skip+warn below it).
Seed-invariant splitter short-circuit to 1 repeat present in both multiseed wrappers.

Global _any_base_monotone flag gates raw baseline fold-scheme for all specs; real cross-scheme gate mismatch. Fix = per-fold-scheme raw baseline (shuffled+TSS, cached, ~2x); needs restructure + regression test.

Composite CV scores only domain-valid rows; raw/sibling score different row populations -> baseline-mismatch leak. Fix = emulate production median fallback on domain-invalid val rows (new fold-scoring logic); changes numerics, needs test.

_oof_* pass-2 specs reference columns living only in local df_aug; suite _build_full_column_from_splits (core/_misc_helpers.py:172) silently returns all-NaN -> garbage composite trained. Opt-in. Real fix = persist OOF recipe + rebuild before training (architectural); cheap interim = WARN on all-NaN.

Residual pass-2 specs auto-merged into specs_ and trained vs RAW y with residual-fitted params; discovered_on_residual (non-field setattr) read only in a test, not in suite routing. Opt-in (default False). Real fix = residual-aware training path or don't merge into specs_.
X_train slice hoisted above the per-spec loop.
Finite-row masking for NaN lag bases in stepwise path (per integrator already-fixed list).

Silent GroupKFold->KFold fallback when n_groups<cv_folds, no log. Real fix = GroupKFold(n_splits=min(cv_folds,n_groups)) for n_groups>=2 + WARN; changes fold construction (numeric), needs validation. Cheap interim = WARN only.

Groups silently win over time_aware; _tiny_cv_rmse_y_scale lacks the cv_splitter escape hatch its raw sibling has. Real fix = grouped forward-chaining splitter (new) + cv_splitter param. Cheap interim = WARN once.
early_stop_threshold serial early-stop has no caller (always inf). Wiring needs raw-baseline threshold threaded into rerank multiseed calls + test; serial-path-only. Shipped-but-unwired class.

Per-seed arrays drop failed seeds without identity -> paired Wilcoxon can mis-pair (consumed at _tiny_rerank.py:676-692). Fix = fixed-length arrays with NaN for failed seeds + diff on jointly-finite; changes multiseed return contract + gate, needs test.

Raw per-bin baseline _tiny_cv_rmse_raw_y call omits time_aware while the global raw baseline (495/515) passes it -> inconsistent estimator on monotone bases. Default-off (per_bin_n_bins=0), bit-identical when off; consistency fix not a tuning choice.

ENS-P2-5 per-bin fallback recompute calls _tiny_cv_rmse_y_scale without time_aware/groups, so cached (TSS/GroupKFold) and recomputed (shuffled KFold) per-bin are different estimators. Default-off, bit-identical when off.
_is_monotone_nondecreasing misses descending time-like bases (needs row-reversal before TSS) and misfires on constant arrays. Descending handling non-trivial; constant-exclusion alone is partial.

Stacked OOF always shuffled KFold; composite_oof_predictions time_aware/cv_splitter never passed -> future-contaminated pass-2 bases/residual. Opt-in. Fix = thread time-awareness through both fit_stacked* + the OOF helper.

ranked[:max(1,len(ranked))] is a no-op slice; residual variant has no pass-1 cap (sibling caps at 3). Honest fix needs a new config field stacked_max_pass1_specs_to_aggregate (hardcoding 3 changes aggregation without a knob).

Forward-stepwise CV is group-blind even when discovery is group-aware (_fit.py:742) -> per-group-constant bases show phantom gains. Fix = add groups param + GroupKFold path + pass _group_ids_for_rerank; new param + splitter path, needs test.
Per-call float64 pool re-materialisation + per-trial column_stack churn. Perf; needs measurement + preallocated trial buffer refactor.
bayesian_alpha_fit_bootstrap(n_bootstrap=0) -> np.percentile(empty) raises; no ci_level range check. Safe input validation.

Polars branch of detect_cat_columns omits the int-as-cat heuristic the pandas branch (348-359) and the sibling detect_group_column_candidates polars branch (190-207) both have. detect_cat_columns is export-only (no production consumer) -> no live blast radius. Parity fix.
Pandas candidate filters exclude bool dtype (is_numeric True, is_integer False) while polars includes it; docstring (313-316) promises bool. Group-detector + cat-detector parity (cat is export-only).

info_bonus=n_unique/(log1p(n_unique)+1) is monotone-increasing, contradicting its comment; ID-like int columns rank first. Fix = unimodal bonus or sparsity penalty -> needs a benchmark to set the peak/threshold + a biz_value test.
Strict diffs>0/<0 rejects timestamp columns with duplicate ticks (normal in event data); sibling _is_monotone_nondecreasing accepts ties. Export-only func. Accept weak monotonicity, exclude constant.

Datetime/temporal columns pass the detect_cat_columns default filter (neither float nor numeric) -> target-encoding a date is a time-leak trap. Export-only func. Exclude temporal dtypes from defaults; reachable via explicit candidate_columns.

cv_selector_mode/alpha/confidence/quantile_level honoured by forward stepwise (_fit.py:710-713) but the rerank multiseed calls never pass them -> always 'mean'. Default cv_selector_mode='mean' -> bit-identical at default; consistency fix.
CatBoost tiny model lacks allow_writing_files=False; parallel fold/spec fits race on catboost_info/ (Windows file-lock -> silent NaN folds) and litter CWD. Bit-identical to model math.

Zero-base baseline sentinel is full-sample np.std(y), not CV-RMSE of the train-fold-mean predictor; inflates first-base gain under TSS on trending y. Only seed_bases=None. Changes the baseline value -> selection; needs measurement.

Early-NaN returns ignore return_per_bin, returning a bare nan where the contract (and the function tail) returns (nan, full(n_bins,nan)). Caller unpacks a per-seed array as per-bin. Benign today but fragile; fix the contract on the early-exit lines.
Conjugate bayesian_alpha_fit lacks the 'caller must filter to the valid domain' contract its bootstrap sibling documents (line 237); NaN y/base silently yields NaN posterior.
Polars n_unique() counts null as a distinct value; pandas nunique() excludes NaN -> off-by-one at the max_unique boundary in the WIRED detect_group_column_candidates.
Docstring requires all candidate arrays to have len(y_train); a mismatch surfaces later as an opaque column_stack ValueError.

Test gap for stacked pass-2 suite TRAINING (would catch A6/A7) + wilcoxon-power floor (A2, now resolved) + multiseed-under-monotone variation (A3, now resolved). Pass-2 training tests can only land alongside the A6/A7 FUTURE fixes; the A2/A3 regression tests should land with confirmation of those resolved items.
In the fixed-id list; verified fix present (polars .to_list() content hash at 376-377, pandas content hash at 442, hash_pandas_object fingerprint at 175-184).
In the fixed-id list; verified fix present: df.slice(0,n_take).hash_rows() (slice-before-hash) at cache.py:162.

Real residual blind spot: polars prefix-only (no tail), pandas still n_edge=8. Widening invalidates the on-disk digest and needs a coverage/cost decision on edge size; document as a known sampling blind spot. Not a quick safe edit.
Sidecar .sha256 removal runs unconditionally even when the value-file os.remove raised (Windows lock) -> surviving entry permanently unverifiable (strict refuses it forever).

Persistent corrupt/tampered entry returns default silently every run -> unbounded silent multi-minute recomputes with no operator signal, while the oversize path right above (711) correctly warns. Known silent-error-swallowing bug class.

pandas _col_stats int branch runs np.unique(arr) (full O(n log n) sort per int column) -- the exact cost the polars branch deliberately dropped for null=. Digest-shape change accepted as it was for polars (one-time cache invalidation).

Drift detector tests alpha only; a pure intercept/level-shift drift never refits yet biases every prediction. Needs a beta/Chow test + a biz_value synthetic (pure-level-shift must refit) + a measured threshold.

Refit uses the FULL rolling buffer (pre+post-drift mixed) -> lagged blend biased toward the dead regime. Fix is change-point detection (two-segment SSE split) -- an algorithmic change needing measurement.
extend(y_arr.tolist()) boxes + np.asarray(deque) unboxes the whole 10k buffer every update -- O(buffer_n) Python work per call. Ring-buffer rewrite is a perf refactor needing a benchmark.

to_audit_trail prints the legacy target__transform__base name that no longer matches spec.name (rendered at report 308) -> two ids for one spec; no extra_base_columns field -> multi-base provenance incomplete (spec.extra_base_columns exists, spec.py:56).

logratio inverse formula hardcodes 10*mad_eff and says 'softcap', but the real inverse (transforms/linear.py:123-129) uses fitted soft_cap_k (default 10) with a hard np.clip; wrong if k!=10 and misleads a reader reproducing the inverse.

Formula/description table covers 4 of ~36 registered transforms; the rest render a generic stub, contradicting the 'reproduce the inverse without source' promise. Right fix is a registry-driven formula field (or extending the dict to the alpha/beta family + chains) -- an extension.

report_to_markdown(random_state=42) default fabricates a load-bearing 'discovered using random_state=42' claim when the real seed differed (docs callers omit the kwarg) -- same trust-failure class as a hallucinated citation.

if not matplotlib.get_backend(): is dead -- get_backend() resolves the sentinel and never returns falsy, so the documented headless Agg-selection never runs; comment misquotes conftest (which does an UNCONDITIONAL use('Agg')).
ax.boxplot(..., tick_labels=names) needs matplotlib>=3.9 but the project pins >=3.7 -> TypeError on 3.7/3.8; set_xticklabels(names,...) at 393 already labels version-independently (redundant kwarg).

Test gap already closed: this file adds same-process string/datetime stability (pandas+polars), cross-subprocess stability under PYTHONHASHSEED randomisation, and content-change detection -- exactly what S16 demanded, landed with the S1 fix.

Class docstring still says 'Thread-safe for single-process use only; concurrent writers will race' -- contradicted by the filelock/atomic-replace machinery now shipped; and _maybe_filelock returns nullcontext() silently despite the comment promising a one-time WARN.

Eviction total_bytes counts only *.pkl while _discovery_cache_bytes_total counts .pkl+.pkl.sha256 (cap vs reported footprint disagree); orphan *.tmp / .lru.lock never swept by eviction or clear(). Needs a reconcile decision + an age-threshold sweep (the clear() tmp/lock sweep sub-part is cheap but the accounting fix needs a direction choice).

composite_id uses json.dumps(..., default=str); array params (median_residual bin_edges, monotonic knots, spline coefs) stringify via numpy repr -> truncate beyond printoptions threshold and obey global printoptions -> id not stable / collides.

Ensemble weight lookup breaks at first nm.startswith(spec.name+'#') -> reports one model's weight as the spec's, but components are f'{spec_name}#{i}' over multiple models (core/_phase_composite_post_xt_ensemble/__init__.py:206); the spec's true mass is the SUM. startswith also prefix-collides.

__all__ exports only the deprecated alias plot_mi_gain_with_ci, not the canonical plot_mi_gain_with_jitter (257) -> star-import users reach it only by the deprecated name; header docstring says 'Four plot helpers' but __all__ lists 8.

Spearman via np.argsort(np.argsort(...)) gives ties arbitrary distinct ranks instead of average ranks -> biased rank-corr in the family-disagreement heatmap when two specs are redundant (equal RMSE). NOTE: the finding's 'scipy already imported here' is FALSE -- scipy is not imported in this module (it is a hard project dep), so the patch must add the import.
x = np.arange(len(window_indices)) with len != len(alpha_arr) raises a cryptic matplotlib 'x and y must have same first dimension' with no hint which arg is wrong.

from .transforms import _TRANSFORMS_REGISTRY imported and never used (verified: sole reference is the import line). Dead + mildly misleading (implies formulas are registry-driven; they are not -- see S12).

compute_config_signature_v1 folds versions only when library_versions is passed (defaults None); the module recipe (100-110) never mentions it, so a docstring-following R&D user builds keys with no code-version component -> stale spec replay across mlframe upgrades. Needs a _DISCOVERY_CACHE_SCHEMA_VERSION constant + mlframe.__version__ folded unconditionally + bump discipline + cache-invalidation note.
_align_fit_sw applies the fit_mask; all 5 call sites slice sw via it (no prefix slice). Covered by test_composite_ensemble_oof_hygiene_audit.py.
kfold composite branch now carves an eval_set (_carve_inner_eval_split + eval_set=) so ES composite components survive. Test pins it.
outer split is group-aware now: GroupKFold in kfold path, whole-group holdout carve in single-split path.
non-monotone time_ordering is argsort(kind=stable)-sorted into a trailing-slice holdout with a WARN, not silently shuffled.
pandas copy now size-gated: WARN above 2GB; df.copy retained only on small frames (polars path is zero-copy).

Real leak: fold predict reuses spec['fitted_params'] fit on FULL train (holdout incl). transform.fit exists and is cheap, but per-fold refit shifts every composite OOF RMSE; flipping it tilts ensemble weights and needs a biz_value benchmark to confirm it improves selection. Out of scope for a safe bit-stable pass.

_full_key=(cache_key,kfold,random_state) omits holdout_frac, time-mode, external-holdout identity and group_ids; two calls differing only in those serve each other's stale matrix (external-holdout path shares the key too). External cache callers only; cheap to fix, no RAM-rule violation.

Docstring says external holdout is preferred, but the kfold>1 branch returns before the external-holdout check, silently ignoring the supplied frame. Suite never sets both, but the fn is public API. Add a WARN (no control-flow change to the untested both-supplied path).

external_holdout_base_per_spec is accepted (296), passed through (735-736), documented as required (487) but never read in _compute_oof_with_external_holdout (verified). Wiring it as the missing-base fallback (turns silent component drops into successful predictions) is a real feature; deleting it removes a fallback contract the suite already builds data for. Either way is out-of-scope for a safe micro-pass.

Combined weighted fit wrapped in except (TypeError,ValueError):pass; on a length-mismatched sw (sklearn raises ValueError) the model silently trains UNWEIGHTED with no log. Add upfront length validation + debug log on fallback. (Narrowing the catch to TypeError flips currently-silent runs to hard errors -> left out, needs care.)

_transform_via does `except Exception: return X` with zero logging; a failing fitted pre_pipeline.transform makes the OOF refit run on RAW X while deploy predicts through the transformed space - the component's OOF RMSE measures a model never deployed. Add a WARN (the one-side-only-divergence -> visible-drop handling stays FUTURE).

(a) lightgbm branch (116-123) imports the lib then appends nothing - dead cold import. (b) xgb.build_info()['USE_CUDA'] is a BUILD flag; stock PyPI wheels are CUDA-enabled, so a CPU-only host gets a false 'GPU non-determinism' warning. AND it with a real device probe; delete the no-op lightgbm block.

env_signature fully __import__s 9 libs (catboost/lgb/xgb cold-import ~0.5-3s each) just to read __version__; it runs on the predict-path drift check (predict.py:106-110), contradicting the ENS-Low-7 keep-cold-import-off-predict rationale. Use importlib.metadata.version (no module import).

Three docstrings/comments describe a removed dropout-refit path: discard_train_matrix docstring claims the stash is used at predict time and '.refit/dropout methods will raise' (no such method); the cap_inference comment first half ('correct without re-slicing') contradicts its second half (it slices). predict is NO-REFIT. Docstring-vs-code contradiction = safe to correct. (Stop-stashing-by-default is the FUTURE memory/pickle win.)

stacking_aware_gate thresholds RAW NNLS coefficients while the docstring documents min_weight as '5% of the unit weight budget'; when sum(w)!=1 (biased/scaled components) the effective threshold drifts with input calibration. Threshold on the budget share instead - aligns code to its documented contract; the renorm block already exists.

residual_dedup_indices iterates len(oof_rmses) candidates against a KxK corr matrix without checking len==K: a short rmse vector silently drops columns [len..K-1] from both kept and dropped; a long one IndexErrors mid-loop. All sibling entry points length-validate parallel arrays. Pure validation guard.

from_train_metrics keeps a caller-passed train-scale baseline when ranking OOF rmses (only overrides when baseline_oof_rmse exists); train RMSE < OOF RMSE so gains shrink/go negative -> spurious single-best fallbacks vs an apples-to-oranges benchmark. Fixing the baseline scale changes weighting numerics (selection-altering) -> needs care; the notes-relabel (rmse_source='oof'|'train') is the safe sub-fix to land alongside it.

When group_ids is present but the group carve cannot be honoured (uniq.size<4 at line 84, or the cumulative guard at 98 fails), the fn falls through to the group-SPLITTING tail carve at 103-108 - the exact within-group-leakage/under-stopping pathology its own docstring cites (+25% OOF RMSE). 2-3 large groups (per-well panels) is realistic. Skip ES instead of group-blind-splitting (safe leak guard; ES at that scale is noise).

composite_oof_predictions calls kf.split(indices) with no y/groups (GroupKFold raises), forwards fit_kwargs verbatim per fold (full-length sample_weight vs fold rows), and both prod call sites pass neither time_aware nor cv_splitter nor group ids -> temporal OOF stack features leak future info. Needs new params (groups, sample_weight) + per-fold slicing + call-site rewiring = public-API extension. Out of scope for a safe micro-pass.

compute_oof_holdout_predictions returns a 3-tuple (matrix, y_holdout, names) with no holdout row indices (kfold path also drops NaN rows), so the suite cannot align sample_weight to OOF rows; the stack solvers grew sample_weight support but run UNWEIGHTED on weighted-objective targets. Adding a 4th return element + threading sample_weight is an API extension. Out of scope for a safe micro-pass.

No time-respecting K-fold: kfold>1+time downgrades to a single trailing slice, and the suite default oof_holdout_source='kfold' nulls the time signal to keep K folds -> default honest-OOF surface is shuffled K-fold training on future rows for AR targets. Fix is forward-chaining/expanding-window folds + a biz_value AR(1) test asserting honestly-worse OOF RMSE. New feature, needs measurement. Out of scope.

4 of 5 inner-eval-carve call sites hardcode random_state=0 while the kfold raw path threads int(random_state); inconsistent, contrary to the module's derive_seeds philosophy, and partially-frozen randomness corrupts seed-sweep variance. Only the group branch of the carve uses random_state, so the common deterministic-tail path is unchanged; only grouped runs shift.

Unused imports + dead locals (verified by grep: no math./warnings./BaseEstimator/RegressorMixin/ElasticNetCV/train_test_split usage in __init__.py; fold_frac computed-never-used at 569; hashlib/warnings/ElasticNetCV unused in _cross_target.py; stacking.py:121 `order` is dead and its comment describes an unused worst-first strategy). Bit-identical cleanup.

residual_correlation_matrix runs np.corrcoef without np.errstate (the sibling residual_dedup_indices guards the identical call at 118-119), emitting RuntimeWarning: invalid value in divide on constant residual columns; docstring (40) overclaims 'Diagonal is 1.0' - a constant column's diagonal is NaN. Errstate only suppresses the warning, no numeric change.

Too-few-finite-rows and NNLS-failure fallbacks return all-survivors with uniform weights and the module has no logger, so a permanently-degenerate (off) gate is indistinguishable from 'ran and kept everything' (the caller's INFO only fires when survivors<total). Additive logging, no behavior change.

from_linear_stack docstring claims the intercept is 'folded into the bias' and 'predict re-normalises only the magnitudes' - both describe removed behaviour (intercept stored separately and added at 253/540; NO-REFIT predict never renormalises). export_metadata omits is_convex so a consumer cannot tell convex from raw-additive weights. Docstring fix + additive dict keys = safe.

kfold/single-split group_ids guards use `_g_arr.shape[0] >= max(idx)+1`, silently slicing a longer misaligned array (e.g. a full-frame group vector where the train-subset one was expected); the external path and the carve itself use exact ==. Tighten to == n_train (in scope at all three sites); on mismatch the existing None fallback fires (now with a WARN). Common case (exact length) is unchanged.

Verified the hygiene-audit test covers N1/N2/N3 only. Still missing: group_ids+sample_weight together, a pin for the non-monotone-time downgrade, and the cache-key holdout_frac collision (N7). These tests ship with their fixes; N7/N19/N21 are FUTURE and the group+sw test pairs with the N7 cache-key landing, so they belong with the corresponding fix commits, not this safe pass.

Heavy-tail mi_n_strata boost is a real no-op under default mi_sample_strategy='random' (screening.py:558 returns the uniform draw before stratification). The substantive fix (also flip strategy to stratified_quantile) changes which rows are sampled -> changes MI estimates -> needs the biz_value test M1 describes. A bit-identical log-truthfulness sub-fix exists but band-aids the dead corrective mechanism, so the full fix is deferred.

Confirmed in-fold transform leakage: fitted_params fit on all valid train rows (_eval.py:56); t_clean computed over whole sample at line 629 before the KFold split (line 647). Per-fold refit changes numerics and needs the noise-spline regression test.

Confirmed: fit_with_stability_check only varies config.random_state (no row subsample) and stride base_seed+i*7919 (line 223) collides with multiseed stride +s_idx*7919 (_screening_tiny.py:445). Adding M-B row subsampling changes results and needs a unit test.

Confirmed no family-wise multiplicity control; defaults eps_mi_gain=-10/require_beats_raw_baseline=False/mi_gain_bootstrap_n=0 (config:285,434,535) make it latent for default users but real when gates re-enabled. BH/maxT is new selective-inference machinery needing validation.

Confirmed correlation_inflation param exists but every composite call site (_screening_tiny.py:377,791; forward_stepwise.py:136) leaves it 1.0 and config has no knob. Adding the Nadeau-Bengio factor needs a new config field plus per-split-geometry calibration.

Confirmed: discovery fit has no time_ordering param; rerank infers time-awareness from base monotonicity (line 218) which never fires for non-cumulative lag-of-y bases, so shuffled KFold leaks on temporal data. Adding a time-signal input threaded from the suite is an architectural change.
Confirmed 'regression only here'. Classification/init_score margin-offset is a new estimator plus spec family plus biz_value test -- an extension.
Confirmed OOF holdout infra exists (compute_oof_holdout_predictions) and zero conformal/predict_interval code anywhere in composite. Split-conformal intervals are a new feature plus public API.

Confirmed detect_time_column_candidates/sort_df_by_time_column are imported/re-exported (discovery/__init__.py:32-33) but never invoked; the 3 time-series transforms stay excluded. Wiring needs sort+screen+index-map plumbing plus measurement; paired with M6.

Early-stop bound sum_so_far>thr*cv_folds only guarantees the MEAN exceeds thr, but the final aggregate uses aggregate_fold_scores(mode=cv_selector_mode) (line 791); for median/low-quantile modes a truncated run can wrongly pass the gate. Latent today (rerank threads neither a finite threshold nor a non-mean mode) but a real soundness bug; guard is bit-identical under default cv_selector_mode=='mean'.

Confirmed m-out-of-n bootstrap: replicate fits on sample_size=subsample_n rows (line 284) but std/percentiles are returned raw (lines 293-299) without the sqrt(m/n) width rescale, overstating CI width ~sqrt(n/m). Standard closed-form correction, deterministic, bit-identical for the default subsample_n=None (m==n); no internal prod caller passes subsample_n.

Confirmed derive_seeds defined but zero call sites (only the def matches derive_seeds(). Actually wiring it into the 6 randomness sources changes every draw -> not bit-identical, needs validation. Minimal safe step: a docstring note that it is currently provided for external callers and not yet threaded into discovery, so the docstring stops implying production wiring.

Confirmed greedy step picks argmin(aggregated) and gates on relative point-estimate gain; per-fold arrays persisted (line 164) but unused for a paired test. Paired majority-of-folds / sign-test selection changes which bases are picked -> needs biz_value/regression validation.
Dead x_screen_valid copy now gated on _need_x_screen_valid; prebinned slice gated on valid_screen.all(). In orchestrator resolved list and verified in code.
finite.all()/col_valid.all() no-copy gates landed in _mi_to_target_prebinned. In resolved list.
_mi_per_feature_prebinned + _aggregate_mi_per_feature decomposition and np.delete removal landed. In resolved list and verified.
_rerank_inner_n_jobs = max(1, cpu//rerank_n_jobs) threaded into _build_tiny_model. In resolved list and verified.
Build _x_full once then per-base np.delete; raw baseline reuses x_full. In resolved list and verified.
_extract_column_array now accepts rows= and gathers O(rows). In resolved list.
_safe_abs_corr_all allocation fix committed. In resolved list.
_row_order_fingerprint slices before hash_rows. In resolved list.

Per-seed redundant domain_check+forward+x_train_matrix[valid] copy is real, but the fix is a prepare()/run(seed) refactor of a hot multi-callsite function and needs measurement. The standalone valid.all() no-copy gate (mirror raw-y 243-249) is bit-identical but only removes one of three copies, not the per-seed re-forward.

Per-base raw per-bin refit is bin_var-independent, but per_bin_n_bins=0 (OFF by default) and the fix needs a _tiny_cv_rmse_raw_y(bin_vars=dict) contract change + per-fold-prediction plumbing. Out of scope for a safe quick pass.

early_stop_threshold is genuinely dead-wired (always inf; no src caller passes it). Behavior is currently correct, just misses the 30-66% serial-fold saving. Activating it requires reordering _tiny_model_rerank to compute the raw baseline before the per-spec loop + a regression test - a restructure, not a micro-guard.

x_boot = x_screen_valid[idx_b] runs every bootstrap replicate but is read only on the non-prebinned else branch; on the default prebinned path it is a dead full-matrix gather. Bootstrap OFF by default. Bit-identical move.

Residual-std probe at full-train scale (2 float64 gathers + transform.forward over all train rows) per work item. Moving it to the screen sample removes the heavy forward but changes the rows the std-ratio is measured on (sample vs full train) -> not bit-identical, needs measurement to confirm the 0.001 gate is unchanged. The bit-identical sub-fix (reuse the line-56 y_train[valid]/base_train[valid] gathers) only saves two gathers, not the dominant forward.

Alpha-drift loop (535) and alpha~1-collapse loop (644) re-extract full base columns, but self._auto_base_pool[s.base_column] (set at 367 = base_full[train_idx]) already holds them; idx1/idx2 = train_idx[:half]/train_idx[half:], so the drift gathers are pool[:half]/pool[half:]/pool. Bit-identical (same float32 values).

int64->int16 prebin codes is bit-identical on MI values (with int32/int64 upcast in _mi_from_binned_pair combo) but spans 3 sites (screening.py 294/296 + _auto_base.py 403-422 + combo widening) and the finding itself requires a gather/bincount benchmark to confirm the cache-effect speedup. Needs measurement.

Two np.unique(g, ...) calls on the identical array in _carve_inner_eval_split; the second (line 89) discards uniq+first_idx and keeps only counts_orig, all in the SAME sorted order. Merge into one call. Bit-identical. Finding cited ensemble/__init__.py:266 but the fn is in _oof_split.py.
y_train_for_strat = y_full[train_idx] duplicates y_train = y_full[train_idx] already computed at line 221; one train-sized float32 copy per fit wasted. Used only at line 294. Bit-identical.

Shrunk-domain mi_y_compare recomputed per transform sharing an identical valid_screen mask. A (base, hash(valid_screen.tobytes())) memo is a real optimization (extension) but needs thread-safe population (rerank runs threaded) and measurement of the ~0.2-0.5s/mask saving. Out of scope for a safe quick pass.

np.nanquantile(col, q_edges) per column even when all-finite (col_finite already computed at 299, guaranteed >=5*nbins at 300). nanquantile is slower than quantile on NaN-free data. Gate on col_finite.all(); use np.quantile(col, ...) on the full column in the fast path (nanquantile drops NaN but NOT inf, so on the all-finite branch np.quantile(col,...) == np.nanquantile(col,...) bit-for-bit, and the else branch preserves current inf behavior).
In the integrator's fixed-id list (iter_transform multi-base crash).

Current predict_quantile already has the `if transform.requires_base: ... else: base_arr=None` branch + zeros placeholder (lines 283-308,321-322); unary cbrt_y no longer crashes. Claim describes pre-fix code.

All three transform.inverse() calls omit groups, so linear_residual_grouped raises 'groups kwarg is required'. _predict_unclipped threads it (64-72); predict_quantile does not. Safe mirror, no numerics change.

_reciprocal_residual_inverse y=1/(t+1/base) is strictly decreasing in t; reciprocal_residual IS in the default transforms list (config:108). predict_quantile guards only ratio/logratio -> silent quantile flip. Minimal safe fix: raise like the ratio guard; full monotone-metadata reversal is FUTURE.
Package docstring lists Discovery + Cross-target ensembling as 'Out of scope ... future PR' yet both are re-exported (lines 180,258). help() misinforms. Pure docstring correction.

No __all__, 96 underscore re-exports, read-only TRANSFORMS_REGISTRY proxy not re-exported. Adding __all__ + re-export reshapes the public star-import surface across many callers; needs a deliberate surface decision + test, not a quick safe pass. DX21 is the cheap subset.

Docstrings promise legacy import paths that don't exist: naming.py 'composite_transforms ... resolves transparently' and discovery/__init__.py:455 'prefer ... composite_auto_detect'. find_spec confirms None -> ModuleNotFoundError if followed. Docstring caveat fix across composite/** headers.

README composite section shows only the manual wrapper; discovery/ensemble/cache/kill-switch/doc-links absent. Documentation expansion only (add Tier-1 snippet + links to composite_targets.md and the tutorial).

Doc claims 'all four core transforms are tried' + transforms=[diff,ratio,logratio,linear_residual]; real default is the 24-entry list (_composite_target_discovery_config.py:82-110). Doc inaccuracy only; state the real set or point at list_transforms().
Line reads 'pre-commit installР С–РЎР‚Р ВµРЎРѓРЎРѓ' (stray Cyrillic 'Р С–РЎР‚Р ВµРЎРѓРЎРѓ' glued on); copy-paste of the dev-setup block fails. Trivial text fix.

Class docstring Parameters omits runtime_stats_callback, auto_variance_stabilise, base_columns, group_column (only inline __init__ comments at 132-175); fit (406) has no docstring. Docstring-only additions hoisting existing inline text; no code change.

residual_dedup_indices is production-wired (CT-ensemble build) with zero tests; keep-best-first logic + min_keep floor + NaN-row guard can silently regress. Writing the 4 proposed tests is real test work (new file + fixtures), not a quick safe code edit. Queue as test-debt.

Three docstrings (composite/__init__.py:47, estimator/__init__.py:45,57) describe the y-clip as multiplicative [Q001/10,Q999*10]; impl is span-based low=q_low-0.9*span, high=q_high+9*span (estimator/__init__.py:78-79). Code is intended-correct; docstrings wrong -> DOC.

Module docstring says '11 transforms' but registry holds 32; TAG_EXTENDED comment 'placeholder; future presets may add more' is stale (tag is load-bearing on 20+ transforms, is the list_transforms filter axis). Doc/count fix; prefer deferring to list_transforms() to avoid drift.

update/get_buffer_state/predict_pre_clip/get_booster + 5 delegated properties still runtime-bound (701-717), invisible to mypy/help(). Adding in-body stubs + class-body property wrappers is a broader surface change with rebinding-order risk; out of scope for a quick safe pass.

Monolith-era imports (contextlib, math, re, warnings, dataclass, field, timer, typing block, numpy, pandas, BaseEstimator, RegressorMixin, clone) are all unused; file is pure re-exports below. Safe deletion.

Unary specs are named via compose_target_name(target_col,transform_name,base) unconditionally (_eval.py:288) -> y-cbrtY-<base> with a spurious base segment, contradicting the registry comment ('no base segment, e.g. y-cbrtY'). Stripping the base changes spec.name/base_column -> cache-key/provenance/dedup ripple, NOT a safe quick edit. Cheapest correct action: fix the registry comment to match current behaviour.

Docs smoke test pins only CompositeTargetEstimator; composite_targets.md/tutorial symbols (CompositeTargetDiscoveryConfig, CompositeSpec, report_to_markdown, MLFRAME_DISABLE_COMPOSITE) unpinned despite the test's docstring claiming tutorial coverage. New test additions = test work; queue it.

94-field config with no generated knob reference; dict-config acceptance undocumented. Headline deliverable is a scripts/ doc generator + doc-drift test (tooling/architectural) -> out of scope for a quick pass. (1-line dict-config docstring note is DOC-able but not the main fix.)

_extract_base docstring + TypeError advertise structured-ndarray support that has no branch; a structured ndarray hits the same TypeError telling you to pass one. Drop the false claim (cheaper + safer than implementing a new branch).
CompositeTargetDiscovery is exported but its required CompositeTargetDiscoveryConfig is not -> every example needs two import locations; invisible to dir(...). Safe additive lazy re-export.

Composite tests split across tests/training/composite/ (38) and tests/training/test_composite_* (28); pytest tests/training/composite runs ~60%. git mv is churny relocation; safe low-risk action is to DOCUMENT the canonical selector (pytest -k composite) and flag the split.
On the already-fixed list; code confirms the per-model hook now slices _y_train_arr = _y_full_arr[train_idx] (train-only clip envelope, no test-y leak).

CONFIRMED real: raw-only CT_ENSEMBLE synthesis still gates on the GLOBALLY-empty specs dict (not composite_specs_by_target_type), so in a mixed suite an extreme-AR-skipped target (no specs entry) gets no lag-floor ensemble while a discovered sibling has specs. Fix = per-target fill of empty [] specs for every regression target with trained models; behavior change touching synthesis + per-target loop, needs a multi-target regression test. Not a quick safe guard.
On the already-fixed list; code confirms the train-RMSE proxy is now lazy via _compute_train_rmse_proxy + _defer_train_proxy, computed only when OOF is deferred/failed.

On the already-fixed list (honoring the input directive). Note: code still sets _time_ordering=None for the default kfold path at :361; the time-aware OOF handling is treated as addressed/committed per the directive.

Stacking-aware gate prunes _pred_matrix/_oof_components/_oof_names/_oof_rmses but NOT _oof_pred_matrix; the OOF gate at :799 then computes _oof_pred_matrix * _w_full[None,:] with pruned _w_full -> ValueError swallowed at :853, silently disabling the OOF gate + AR(1) failsafe. Confirmed against current code.

Per-spec base matrices keyed by spec['base_column'] collide when a multi-base and a single-base spec share a primary base; last writer wins -> the other spec's OOF refit raises a base-width mismatch and is silently excluded. Keying by unique spec['name'] is bit-identical when no collision. Lookup side at composite/ensemble/__init__.py:320,596,859 also keys by base_column.

CONFIRMED: any single component fold failure / non-finite returns None for the whole MTR weighting (-> equal-mean, forfeiting the benched ~9% win), all logged at DEBUG. Per-component exclusion changes the return contract (caller at _post_xt_ensemble_mtr.py:265 injects weights shaped to ALL components) and must re-validate against bench_mtr_nnls_oof.py -- architectural, not a quick guard. Cheap partial available: bump the three log sites (lines 74,85 + __init__.py:133-139) to WARNING.

y-scale verdict branch reads only the val metric; when y-scale entries carry no finite val metric _best_val stays None -> best_model='-' even though test metrics exist. The else branch already has the documented test-fallback (:104-118, added after a prod incident). Same incident shape, untreated branch. Fix only flips '-'->value, no numerics.

Per-model hook emits the TEST y-scale chart (one full-test predict/entry) at body:894; the wrap-pass skip_predict=True branch re-emits the SAME chart (same _yscale_{composite} path -> overwrite) for every entry -> duplicate predict + file write + log. Confirmed.

_lag1_ar is the SINGLE picked-target stat (set as picked_target_name at _main_train_suite_target_distribution.py:268) applied to EVERY regression target with no name check -> over-skips non-AR siblings / under-protects AR siblings. picked_target_name is in the report so the guard is free. Same pattern in the MLP twin extreme_ar_skip_decision (mlp_helpers:192-229).

CONFIRMED: group-aware tiny-rerank is gated on the analyzer RECOMMENDATION (knob_overrides.split_config.prefer_group_aware) not the ACTUAL splitter config; a user who supplies group_ids + a group-aware splitter without the analyzer recommending it gets plain-KFold rerank (the documented prod failure). Fix needs a real 'splitter was group-aware' flag threaded from the suite boundary -- wiring/architectural, not a local guard.

Three silent-swallow gaps: (1) fit-exception (:541) and (2) row-mismatch (:319) log-only with NO composite_target_failures entry (every other skip path records one); (3) cache-replay `except Exception: _cached_specs=[]` (:451) has ZERO logging and leaves a metadata/behaviour divergence (specs claimed at :421, no T columns added). All cheap, no numerics.

pd.concat([sub, target_series], axis=1) copies blocks under pandas<3 / no-CoW -- a full peak-RAM duplication of the (100+GB-convention) train frame per regression target. Real, but the prescribed fixes (mutate-and-restore try/finally, or a y= kwarg into CompositeTargetDiscovery.fit) change a deliberately-anti-mutation path and need a before/after RAM measurement per perf-measure-first. Out of scope for a safe quick pass.

_LagPredictDeployableModel.predict returns the lag column verbatim incl. NaN at group starts; the OOF helper raises 'non-finite holdout predictions' (composite/ensemble/__init__.py:681) -> lag_predict dropped from the surviving set on EXACTLY the strong-AR grouped targets it exists for. Imputing non-finite with a fit-time train-median is a self-contained, clearly-correct edge guard (NaN preds are never useful; no copy/bench). Also fixes deploy-time NaN-lag rows.

CONFIRMED: both watchdog blocks build a 1-D base from the single base_column; for linear_residual_multi (in _ADDITIVE_TRANSFORMS, :548) inverse/forward need the (n,1+K) matrix -> ValueError swallowed at DEBUG (:587,:650) -> ZERO watchdog coverage for the spec family most prone to wrapper-math bugs. Fix = build the multi-column base in both blocks + a new regression test that corrupts a multi-base wrapper and asserts the WARNING fires. Test-gap + 2-site matrix build, beyond a one-line guard.

is_composite_target_name(model_name) runs on the concatenated f'{target_name} {model_name} {cur_target_name}' (passed at _phase_train_one_target_model_setup.py:539), so a raw target containing '-diff-'/'-ratio-' is mis-stamped MTRESID. Reporting-only; select_target has exactly ONE caller and cur_target_name is available there.

_mttr/_mtts are computed on the y TEST slice (y_target) but the chart header renders 'MTTR/MTTS=' (suite convention: MTTR = TRAIN-split mean) -> cross-reading against MTTR= log lines gives inconsistent numbers under drift. Also _human_inner (:64) is computed and never used.

recover_composite_y_scale_metrics is annotated `-> dict[int, np.ndarray]` but delegates to _run_composite_target_wrapping whose returned cache is keyed by (id(wrapper), id(frame), shape) tuples (wrapping.py:245,457).

'wrapped %d model(s)' logs len(_entries), counting entries that had no predict, were already wrapped (continue at :294), or failed from_fitted_inner (continue at :318) -> operator can be told 'wrapped 4' when 1 stayed T-scale.

CONFIRMED parity gaps vs single-target: (a) MTR ensemble is APPENDED into models[tt][orig_tname] (:281) not a dedicated _CT_ENSEMBLE__{name} key -> it appears alongside its own component siblings (votenrank/per-entry tables misread it); (b) SimpleNamespace lacks metrics={} (single-target sets it at __init__.py:880); (c) no ensembles_chosen['cross_target'] stamp. (a) is a registration-shape change needing downstream save/predict verification; (b)/(c) are cheap but best landed with (a).

Class docstring (:31 'Weights are normalised to sum to 1') and the weights property doc (:108-112 'Each column sums to 1.0 ... post-normalisation (nnls)') contradict the code, which DELIBERATELY keeps RAW NNLS weights (:162-174 rationale). A maintainer trusting the doc could re-add normalisation and regress fits.

When filtered_train_idx is None, _y_arr[filtered_train_idx] becomes _y_arr[None] -> shape (1,n), so the guard fires the confusing 'y[1] vs filtered_train_df[N]' instead of stating the index is missing. The BaselineDiagnostics block 5 lines up (:279-281) already handles None explicitly.

_arr[~_fin] = median(finite) imputes a component whose train predict FAILED with the MEDIAN RMSE -> from_train_metrics grants it mid-pack weight on the fallback path. Safer = impute max(finite) (worst) or drop. Only reachable on the double-failure path (OOF failed AND component scoring failed); changing imputation alters fallback weights so wants a small eyeball/bench before flipping. Low reach, low value-per-risk for the quick pass.

Verified dead (grep: appear only on import lines, no call site): SimpleNamespace, report_model_perf, get_transform, compute_oof_holdout_predictions, PrePipelinePredictShim, _CrossEns, CompositeTargetEstimator, format_suite_end_summary, _build_full_column_from_splits, _entry_metric, _fmt, _short_tag_fn, _strip, _DEFAULT_OOF_RANDOM_STATE, _PROB_NORM_EPS. No external module imports these from here (only run_composite_post_processing + _run_suite_end_dummy_baselines_summary). CORRECTION: keep `np` (used in annotations :68,:139) and the three noqa-F401 re-exports.

The dummy-floor / prescreen / oof_weighted gates read the strongest-dummy primary_metric generically and compare against component RMSEs; today the regression primary is hard-coded val_RMSE (baselines/_dummy_metrics_pick_plot.py:108) so units match. The mixed-units risk only materialises if a non-RMSE regression primary is introduced -- a config path that does not exist. Document the RMSE-family assumption at the gate sites rather than add speculative endswith guards for an unreachable case.
